Eligibility & Age Limits: Entry age and cover term as per base policy (typically adult lives; cover generally ceases by age 70); rider term cannot exceed base term.
Applicable Base Policies: Only under eligible base policies that specifically allow LIC’s Accident Benefit Rider.
Rider Sum Assured Limits: Minimum AB Sum Assured usually Rs 10,000; maximum linked to base Sum Assured and overall per-life AB limit set by LIC.
Premium Basis & Modes: Premium is payable along with base policy premium in the same mode and for the same premium-paying term (regular or limited) as the base policy.
Benefit Description: On accidental death of the Life Assured while rider is in force, an additional Accident Benefit Sum Assured is paid as lump sum in addition to the base death benefit; no benefit for natural death or disability.
Waiting / Survival Periods: No separate waiting or survival period; accidental death must meet policy definition and usually occur within a specified period (for example within 180 days) from the accident as per policy text.
Exclusions: Standard accident exclusions: self-inflicted injury or suicide attempt, intoxication, war or war-like operations, aviation other than as fare-paying passenger, participation in criminal or hazardous activities and other exclusions as stated in the rider document.
Limitations & Conditions: Rider pays only on accidental death; must be attached at inception or within allowed window; has to be in force at time of accident; cannot exceed limits specified for the base plan.
Paid-up Rules: Does not acquire paid-up value; cover under this rider stops if base policy is in lapsed or paid-up condition as per rider rules.
Surrender Value: No independent surrender value payable under the rider.
Revival Rules: Rider can be revived only along with revival of the base policy within the period allowed (normally 5 years) subject to LIC’s underwriting and interest on arrears.
Loan Availability: No loan is available under the rider.
Multiple Rider Attachment Allowed?: Yes; may be attached with other riders subject to base-product rules and overall rider limits.
Interaction with Base Policy: Accident Benefit Sum Assured is paid over and above the base policy death benefit; does not affect bonuses or other base-plan benefits.
Termination Rules: Ends on earliest of: payment of AB claim, expiry of rider term, termination/surrender/paid-up of base policy, cancellation on free-look, or termination for fraud/misrepresentation as per Insurance Act provisions.
Free Look Period: 30 days (same as base policy free-look for rider endorsement).

Eligibility & Age Limits: Proposer (payer) entry 18–55 years (nearest birthday); rider term linked to child’s age and outstanding premium paying term; risk on rider ceases at earlier of end of rider term or child attaining 25 years.
Applicable Base Policies: Child-type base policies where proposer is different from Life Assured and where PWB rider is allowed by LIC.
Rider Sum Assured Limits: No separate Sum Assured; benefit is waiver of future premiums on death of proposer for remaining PPT (subject to rider term limits).
Premium Basis & Modes: Premium for the rider is payable along with base policy premium in same mode (Y/HY/Q/M) up to rider expiry or proposer’s death; rate based on proposer’s age and term.
Benefit Description: On death of proposer while rider and base policy are in force, all future premiums of the base policy falling due after date of death are waived till the end of rider term; policy continues with full benefits; no maturity or death benefit is payable under the rider itself.
Waiting / Survival Periods: No special waiting period except suicide clause on proposer; benefit applies from commencement if death meets conditions.
Exclusions: Suicide of proposer within 12 months from commencement or revival usually leads only to refund of a portion of rider premiums; deaths not meeting definition; exclusions per policy document.
Limitations & Conditions: Rider waives only base-plan premiums, not premiums of other riders unless specifically stated; applicable only while both base and rider are in force and child is below specified age.
Paid-up Rules: Rider does not acquire paid-up value; ceases if base policy is lapsed, made fully paid-up or surrendered; waiver benefit does not apply once rider is terminated.
Surrender Value: No independent surrender value is payable under this rider.
Revival Rules: Can be revived only along with revival of the base policy within permitted period, subject to LIC underwriting and payment of arrears with interest.
Loan Availability: No loan under the rider (loans, if any, are as per base policy).
Multiple Rider Attachment Allowed?: Yes; can co-exist with other riders on the same base policy subject to product rules.
Interaction with Base Policy: On admissible proposer death, future base-policy premiums are waived but base-plan benefits (death, survival, maturity, bonuses) continue as if premiums were paid; rider itself ends on grant of waiver.
Termination Rules: Rider ends on proposer’s death (after benefit trigger), on child reaching maximum age, on expiry of rider term, on base-policy lapse/surrender/paid-up, or cancellation/free-look.
Free Look Period: 30 days from receipt of policy/rider endorsement.

Rider Name: LIC Accidental Death and Disability Benefit Rider (AD&DB)
Eligibility & Age Limits: Entry: 18–65 years; maximum cover ceasing age 70 years; rider term cannot exceed smaller of base term and (70 – entry age); available only on adult lives.
Applicable Base Policies: Permitted under specified regular, limited and single premium base plans as per LIC’s rider-eligibility list.
Rider Sum Assured Limits: Minimum AB Sum Assured Rs 10,000; maximum usually Rs 100 lakh per life (and up to Rs 200 lakh when counting Jeevan Shiromani as per rider rules); aggregate limits across all policies apply.
Premium Basis & Modes: Premiums payable with the base-policy premium in same mode and for rider term/PPT chosen; level rates by age, term and smoking/gender class as applicable.
Benefit Description: On accidental death: full Accidental Benefit Sum Assured payable as lump sum in addition to base death benefit. On total and permanent disability due to accident (as per detailed definition): AB Sum Assured paid in 10 equal annual monthly instalments over 10 years and further premiums for rider and for an equal amount of base Sum Assured are waived.
Waiting / Survival Periods: Accidental event must occur while rider in force and death/disability must occur within 180 days of accident; disability must be permanent and satisfy Activities of Daily Living / medical criteria; no separate waiting period otherwise.
Exclusions: Standard accident exclusions: self-inflicted injury, attempted suicide, intoxication, war/war-like operations, participation in riots/strikes, aviation other than as passenger, criminal acts, hazardous sports, military/paramilitary duty and other exclusions listed in rider document.
Limitations & Conditions: Benefits restricted to first qualifying accident; disability must meet stringent medical and functional criteria; cover not available on minors; benefit not payable if accident outside risk period or after termination; only one claim (death or disability) allowed under rider.
Paid-up Rules: Does not acquire paid-up value; if base policy becomes paid-up or lapses, rider cover stops and no further AD&DB benefit is available.
Surrender Value: No separate surrender value; in some SP/limited-pay cases, treatment may follow URPV rules linked to base but no independent SV for rider.
Revival Rules: Can be revived only along with base policy revival within allowed period, subject to underwriting and interest on arrears; no benefit for events during lapse.
Loan Availability: No rider-specific loan; policy loans, if any, are governed by base policy only.
Multiple Rider Attachment Allowed?: Yes; may be attached alongside other riders subject to limits under each base plan and overall LIC caps per life.
Interaction with Base Policy: On accidental death, rider Sum Assured is paid over and above base death benefit; on accidental TPD, instalment benefit plus waiver of future premiums for rider and equivalent amount of base SA; base-plan bonuses/benefits continue as per its rules.
Termination Rules: Rider terminates on earliest of full claim payment (death or disability), expiry of rider term, termination/surrender/paid-up/lapse of base policy, free-look cancellation or termination for fraud/misrepresentation as per Section 45.
Free Look Period: 30 days from receipt of rider endorsement and policy.

Eligibility & Age Limits: Typical entry 18–60 years; cover ceasing age usually up to 75 years; rider term cannot exceed base term or maximum age at exit allowed.
Applicable Base Policies: Available with specified participating and non-participating base plans which explicitly allow LIC’s New Term Assurance Rider.
Rider Sum Assured Limits: Rider Sum Assured is chosen by policyholder but cannot exceed Basic Sum Assured under base policy or product-specific caps.
Premium Basis & Modes: Premium charged in addition to base premium; same mode and frequency as base (Y/HY/Q/M); premium-paying term same as base policy PPT or rider term as per product brochure.
Benefit Description: Provides an additional Term Rider Sum Assured payable on death of Life Assured during rider term while policy in force; no maturity, survival or surrender benefit under this rider.
Waiting / Survival Periods: No special waiting period beyond risk commencement; suicide clause may apply as per rider/base terms.
Exclusions: Suicide within 12 months of commencement or revival may limit benefit to a refund formula; other exclusions usually follow base-policy rules and standard LIC terms.
Limitations & Conditions: Pure risk benefit only; rider SA must not exceed Base SA; cannot be added after certain policy duration; no benefit on survival to maturity.
Paid-up Rules: Does not acquire paid-up value; ceases if base policy is lapsed, surrendered or made paid-up as per policy conditions.
Surrender Value: No independent surrender value is payable for the rider.
Revival Rules: Revival possible only along with base policy within allowed revival period on payment of arrears with interest and evidence of insurability.
Loan Availability: No policy loan is available in respect of this rider (loans relate to base only).
Multiple Rider Attachment Allowed?: Yes; can co-exist with other riders like ADB, CI, etc., subject to base-product rules and overall limits.
Interaction with Base Policy: On death of Life Assured, rider SA is paid in addition to base policy death benefit and any other rider benefits; does not affect bonuses or GA under base plan.
Termination Rules: Terminates on earliest of death claim, expiry of rider term, base policy lapse/surrender/paid-up, free-look cancellation or termination for fraud/misrepresentation.
Free Look Period: 30 days (rider endorsement can be cancelled within this period along with base policy).

Rider Name: LIC Linked Accidental Death Benefit Rider
Eligibility & Age Limits: Entry 18–65 years; maximum cover ceasing age 70 years (nearer birthday); rider term at least 5 years and not beyond earlier of base policy term and (70 – entry age).
Applicable Base Policies: Only under specified linked (ULIP) plans of LIC that allow this rider.
Rider Sum Assured Limits: Minimum Rider Sum Assured Rs 10,000; maximum Rs 100 lakh overall per life (Rs 200 lakh when including Jeevan Shiromani as per rider rules); chosen in multiples of Rs 5,000; aggregate across all linked ADB riders considered.
Premium Basis & Modes: No separate premiums; a rider charge is deducted monthly by cancellation of units from ULIP fund; charge rate e.g. Rs 0.40 per Rs 1,000 SA (higher rate for certain occupational risks such as police duty) as per rider schedule; mode of premium for base policy unchanged.
Benefit Description: If death of Life Assured occurs due to accident while rider is in force and within 180 days of the accident, an additional Accidental Death Benefit Sum Assured is paid as lump sum in addition to linked base death benefit; no benefit on natural death or disability; no maturity value.
Waiting / Survival Periods: Death must be caused by an accident as defined and occur within 180 days of such accident; no other waiting period; no survival period requirement beyond death itself.
Exclusions: Excludes self-inflicted injury, suicide attempt, intoxication, war and war-like operations, aviation other than as fare-paying passenger, participation in riots, strikes, criminal acts, hazardous sports, and certain armed forces/police duties; detailed list provided in policy document.
Limitations & Conditions: Benefit only for first qualifying accidental death; rider must be active and not lapsed/discontinued; no disability cover; not available for minors; charges continue as long as rider is in force.
Paid-up Rules: Does not acquire paid-up value; if ULIP is discontinued or made paid-up as per scheme rules, rider ceases accordingly.
Surrender Value: No independent surrender value; on policy surrender or discontinuance, rider ends with no extra value beyond base policy values.
Revival Rules: Revival only along with linked base policy revival/discontinuance rules as per ULIP regulations and LIC underwriting; no cover during discontinuance period until revived.
Loan Availability: No loan available specific to rider; ULIP loans, if any, governed by base contract.
Multiple Rider Attachment Allowed?: Yes; may be attached together with other ULIP riders subject to product design and IRDAI norms.
Interaction with Base Policy: On accidental death, rider SA is paid over and above ULIP fund value or other base death benefit structure; rider charges are deducted from fund while in force; no impact on fund allocation structure except charges.
Termination Rules: Ends on earliest of: payment of accidental death benefit, expiry of rider term, termination/surrender/discontinuance/lapse of base ULIP, free-look cancellation, or termination for fraud/misrepresentation.
Free Look Period: 30 days from receipt of ULIP policy document including rider endorsement.

Eligibility & Age Limits: Entry age 18–65 years (completed/nearer birthday as specified); maximum cover ceasing age 75 years; rider term same as base plan subject to minimum 5 years (regular pay) / 10 years (limited pay) and maximum 25 years or earlier of base term and age 75.
Applicable Base Policies: May be attached only at inception of eligible non-linked life insurance base plans covering adults, as per LIC’s rider-eligibility list; continuance of base cover is a pre-condition.
Rider Sum Assured Limits: Minimum Critical Illness Sum Assured Rs 1,00,000; maximum equal to Sum Assured under base plan subject to base-plan maximum and overall cap of Rs 1,00,00,000 across all CI riders of LIC for that life (including New Critical Illness Benefit Rider).
Premium Basis & Modes: Additional level premium payable along with base policy premium in the same mode (Y/HY/Q/M); regular or limited PPT aligned with base policy within rider rules; no rider premium after earlier of end of base PPT or age 75; taxes extra.
